name: Four Types of Trust
definition: A 2x2 framework mapping trust by (1) who bears the risk and (2) who does the punishing, yielding four trust types: self-risk-other-punishes, other-risk-self-punishes, self-risk-environment-punishes, other-risk-environment-punishes.

name: Core Four Advertising Methods
definition: The only four ways a person can let others know about what they sell, derived from combining two audience types (warm/cold) with two communication modes (one-to-one/one-to-many): warm outreach, posting free content, cold outreach, and paid ads.

name: Lead Getter Hierarchy
definition: Four types of external parties who advertise on your behalf in ascending order of leverage: customers (referrals), employees, agencies, and affiliates; each multiplies lead flow beyond what a single founder can generate.

name: More Better New
definition: A three-stage framework for scaling any advertising channel: first maximize volume (more), then improve conversion rates at the biggest drop-off constraint (better), then expand to new placements, platforms, or core four activities (new).

name: Document-Demonstrate-Duplicate
definition: A three-step employee training model: document every step of the task as a checklist while performing it, demonstrate the checklist step-by-step in front of the trainee, then have the trainee duplicate the process while being observed.

name: Whisper-Tease-Shout Launch Method
definition: A three-phase product and affiliate launch sequence: whisper (create curiosity months out with behind-the-scenes hints), tease (reveal value elements and date weeks out), shout (issue specific CTAs with urgency and scarcity in the final days).

name: What-Who-When Ad Framework
definition: An advertising copywriting structure that combines the eight value elements (dream outcome, perceived likelihood, time delay, effort) with multiple stakeholder perspectives (prospect, spouse, colleagues, rivals) and three time horizons (past, present, future) to generate compelling ad angles.

name: Seven-Step Lead Magnet Creation
definition: A sequential process for building a high-converting lead magnet: identify the narrow problem and right avatar, select solution type (reveal problem / sample / one step of multi-step), choose delivery mechanism (software / information / service / physical product), test naming, maximize ease of consumption, maximize quality, and add a clear CTA.

name: Generate Flow, Monetize Flow, Add Friction
definition: A three-stage acquisition sequencing model: start with the highest-volume (free or deeply discounted) front end to generate lead flow, monetize that flow, then progressively add friction to raise lead quality over time.

name: Four Ways to Display Discounts
definition: A framework for structuring discount offers across four display formats (dollar off, percentage off, buy-X-get-Y, limited-time unit price) so businesses can test which representation drives the highest response rate for a given price point.

name: Five Friction Levers
definition: Five controllable mechanisms for raising free-offer lead quality while reducing volume: increased qualifications, information requirements and question types, number of steps, forced consumption of sales material, and advertisement length.

name: Two-Step Sale
definition: A sales architecture that splits the close into two appointments: a low-cost or discounted first transaction that captures card-on-file and builds trust, followed by a second appointment where the core high-ticket offer is sold as an upsell.

name: Four Offer Types Money Model
definition: A sequenced offer stack combining Attraction Offers (strangers to customers), Upsell Offers (spend more), Downsell Offers (yes when they would say no), and Continuity Offers (keep buying) to maximize 30-day profit and recurring revenue.

name: Payment Plan Downsell Process
definition: A seven-step funnel shifting from full prepayment to free trial: (1) reward prepay with discount, (2) 3rd-party financing/credit card/layaway, (3) half now half later, (4) desire check 1-10 scale, (5) three-payment split, (6) evenly spread payments, (7) free trial with card.

name: Menu Upsell Four-Tactic Process
definition: A structured upsell sequence combining Unselling (cross out what they don't need), Prescription Upselling (tell them exactly what they do need), A/B Upselling (ask preference between two options), and Card On File (make payment frictionless).

name: Anchor Upsell Framework
definition: A five-step premium-first pricing process: present anchor (5-10x premium), get the gasp, come to the rescue by asking what makes the premium matter to them, present core offer, ask how they want to pay.

name: Win Your Money Back Offer
definition: An attraction offer where customers pay upfront and earn their money back as cash or store credit if they meet defined results, actions, or both criteria; the real profit comes from the upsell made to successful customers.

name: Grand Slam Offer
definition: An offer combining an attractive promotion, unmatchable value proposition, premium price, and unbeatable guarantee with a money model that lets you get paid to acquire customers, creating a category of one with no direct price comparison.

name: The Value Equation
definition: A four-variable formula for perceived value: (Dream Outcome x Perceived Likelihood of Achievement) divided by (Time Delay x Effort & Sacrifice). Maximizing the numerators and minimizing the denominators increases willingness to pay.

name: M-A-G-I-C Naming Formula
definition: A five-component offer naming framework: Magnetic reason why + Avatar announcement + Goal statement + Interval (time duration) + Container word (bundle descriptor). Applies 3-5 components to create a name that attracts the target avatar and signals unique value.

name: Sales-to-Fulfillment Continuum
definition: A trade-off axis between ease of selling (high fulfillment burden) and ease of fulfillment (harder to sell); the optimal sweet spot is a highly sellable offer that is also operationally manageable. Start by over-delivering, then systematize.

name: Virtuous Cycle of Price
definition: A causal loop in which premium pricing increases client emotional investment, perceived value, and results, attracting better clients and generating margin to reinvest in service quality, which sustains premium pricing. The inverse vicious cycle applies when prices are cut.

name: Ad Assembly Line Process
definition: Decompose ad creation into three interchangeable components (hook, meat, CTA) produced in bulk separately, then assembled combinatorially to generate 150-750 unique ads per week from a single recording session.

name: Five Ad Meat Formats
definition: Five proven content formats for the body of an ad: Demonstration, Testimonial, Education, Story, and Faceless; these formats work across all industries and can be mixed and matched.

name: Audience Awareness Ladder (adapted from Eugene Schwartz)
definition: A five-level model mapping ad hook style to audience knowledge state: Most Aware (offer-driven), Product-Aware (proof-driven), Solution-Aware (promise-driven), Problem-Aware (pain-driven), Completely Unaware (curiosity-driven).

name: Four Pillars of Lead Nurture
definition: A four-part framework (Availability, Speed, Personalization, Volume) derived from analysis of 4,000+ daily appointments in ALAN software that identifies the primary drivers of show rates for scheduled sales appointments.

name: BAMFAM
definition: Book-A-Meeting-From-A-Meeting: always schedule the next appointment during the current appointment rather than following up later, to prevent leads from going cold between calls.

name: Lead Nurture Throughput Math
definition: Three-metric model: Schedule Rate (engaged leads who schedule) x Show Rate (scheduled leads who show) = Throughput (total engaged leads who show). Optimizing any variable compounds the others.

name: Suffering as Fixed Cost
definition: Suffering is an invariant cost incurred on any life path; the variable is the return you choose to demand for that suffering.
concept: All Life Paths Have Equal Suffering; Outcome Differs by Choice

name: Perceived Lack of Options Model
definition: Sadness is operationally defined as a perceived lack of options; options are expanded not by changing circumstances but by changing the trades one is willing to make.

name: Three-Variable Lead Viability Math
definition: A channel's economic viability is determined by exactly three numbers: cost per lead, close rate (% of leads converted), and average deal size. Their product determines CAC and whether the channel is profitable.

name: Funnel Friction Stacking
definition: When a funnel generates too many unqualified leads, add qualification gates in sequence — first in ad copy, then in opt-in fields (scored questions), then by gating the scheduler behind a score threshold — until the unqualified volume is filtered out.

name: Single-Behavior Leverage Framework
definition: Identify one behavior or skill with the highest number of positive downstream impacts; high leverage means one input produces many large outcomes.

name: Half-Step Frame Repackaging
definition: Keep existing content substance (hooks, stories, topics) unchanged but shift the narrative frame from end-user perspective to practitioner-facing case-study perspective, changing the audience attracted without rebuilding the content library.

name: Four-Component Mental Toughness Model
definition: Mental toughness has four measurable variables: (1) threshold — how much adversity before behavior changes; (2) magnitude — how much behavior changes once it does; (3) resilience — how fast behavior returns to baseline; (4) adaptation — whether the rebound lands above, at, or below the original baseline.

name: Five Levels of Friendship
definition: A hierarchy for evaluating friendship depth: Level 1 = talk about the past; Level 2 = talk about other people; Level 3 = talk about ideas; Level 4 = can do things together; Level 5 = can make, lose, and spend money together.

name: Two-Question Decision Razor
definition: Before acting on an impulse, ask (1) what do I want to have happen? and (2) what action increases the odds of that happening? The initial gut reaction is usually antithetical to both answers.

name: More, Better, New Scaling Hierarchy
definition: Three categories of business moves in descending risk-adjusted return order: More (repeat what works), Better (small variation on what works), New (wholly different action). New is highest resource cost and lowest probability of success; most businesses should limit themselves to one New per year.

name: Anchor Upsell with Downsell Fallback
definition: Present the premium tier (e.g., $100K) as the primary offer and sell it fully before offering a stripped-down version at a lower price; the buyer who declines the premium has already mentally justified its value, making the downsell feel like a bargain.

name: Qualified Questionnaire Funnel
definition: Replace a generic request-for-quote form with a multi-question quiz that captures budget and fit signals; route qualified leads directly to a booking calendar while disqualified leads receive a PDF or no-book outcome.

name: Multi-Stage Business Scaling Roadmap
definition: A 10-stage progression across 8 business functions (product, marketing, sales, customer success, recruiting, IT, human resources, finance) mapped from zero to $100M+, derived from reviewing all Acquisition.com portfolio companies and where they got stuck.

name: Core Four Acquisition Channels
definition: Four mutually exclusive methods for getting customers: paid ads, organic content, cold outreach, and affiliate/partner referral. Every business should map which of the four it currently uses before deciding what to add.

name: Six Pillars of Self-Work
definition: Alex Hormozi's summary of personal development reduced to six interdependent principles: thoughts are not inherently true, feelings do not mandate action, events have no inherent good or bad nature, ignorance is the primary enemy, changing surroundings changes behavior, and actions (not past) define identity.

name: Yeti Can Vacuum Model
definition: A mental model for separating internal emotional state from behavioral output, analogous to a double-walled vacuum insulated can: feelings are on one side, actions on the other, with deliberate space (time) as the insulating layer between them.

name: Value Equation for Affiliate Activation
definition: Three levers for getting affiliates to act: reduce risk (testimonials, ongoing payments), increase speed (urgency window), and increase ease (pre-built emails, landing pages, CRM setup).

name: VSL Pre-Frame Close System
definition: A three-part cold-outbound close architecture: (1) VSL that pre-frames the prospect before the call, (2) scripted qualification and pre-frame on the call, (3) training cadence ensuring reps follow the process — designed to move close rates from 6% to 20%+ on cold leads.

name: Quality + Quantity Offer Bifurcation
definition: Split a project-based service into two distinct offer tracks: a high-ticket upfront quality package capturing representative ICPs, and a lower-cost recurring quantity package providing fresh, ongoing UGC-style assets — enabling continuity revenue that project-only pricing cannot sustain.

name: Software Valuation Components
definition: Three features that make software economically valuable at exit: near-zero marginal cost for additional customers (high gross margin), viral growth, and revenue retention (ideally net negative churn via account expansion).

name: Four-Component Mental Toughness Model
definition: Mental toughness decomposed into four measurable behavioral dimensions: tolerance (how much hardship before behavior changes), fortitude (how severe the behavior change is), resilience (how quickly one returns to baseline), and adaptability (whether the new baseline is higher or lower than the old one).

name: Trauma as Permanent Behavior Change
definition: Trauma defined operationally as a permanent change of behavior resulting from an aversive stimulus, removing the valence assumption; trauma can be positive (new baseline higher) or negative (new baseline lower).

name: Demand Unlock Decision Tree
definition: A three-branch diagnostic for demand-constrained businesses: (1) open a new channel, (2) lower CAC via CRO or better creative/offer, or (3) simply spend more on existing channel.

name: UGC Content Loop
definition: A decentralized content engine in which customers are incentivized to post trip montages in exchange for gated content, generating 20-30 videos per week that are tested organically and the winners are scaled as paid ads.

name: Kaleidoscope Creative Variation
definition: Take one winning creative and generate 20-30 visual variations via AI filters, color treatments, cartoonization, and motion conversion, then blast all variants to find new winners without changing the underlying offer or copy.

name: Lead Scoring by Dual Qualifier
definition: Score and prioritize inbound leads by two qualifying variables (e.g., credit card spend and vacation spend), calling two-for-two matches first, one-for-one matches second, and zero-for-two last.

name: Damaging Admissions Front-Load
definition: Open a sales call by proactively listing every limitation and reason the product will not work for the prospect, before stating benefits, so the prospect self-qualifies and believes the benefits when stated.

name: Custom-Order Sales Flow
definition: A four-stage sequential funnel for converting DIY railing browsers into custom-order buyers: (1) form opt-in, (2) pre-call VSL with price anchoring and BANT qualification via SMS, (3) consultative phone close with on-call discount incentive, (4) card capture on the call.

name: Long-Term Email Nurture Structure
definition: A recurring two-to-three-times-per-week email cadence rotating three content themes: (1) before/after transformation photos, (2) cool project showcases, (3) FAQ objection-breakers — each email structured as subject line, immediate visual reward, CTA link, and PS.
concept: 2x Weekly Email Cadence with Rotating Themes

name: Virtuous Cycle of Pricing
definition: Raising price increases emotional investment, which increases perceived value, which improves results, which decreases customer demandingness and generates more revenue to fund delivery — a self-reinforcing loop.

name: Demand-Constrained vs Supply-Constrained Diagnosis
definition: Before investing in lead generation, determine whether doubling lead flow would be absorbed (demand-constrained) or collapse fulfillment (supply-constrained); only demand-constrained businesses should invest in more leads.

name: Affiliate Offer Architecture (Lead-Magnet Upsell)
definition: Structure affiliate deals so the partner sells or gives away the lower-ticket service, keeps all that revenue, and the affiliate business upsells into its core higher-ticket offer on the back end — converting the partner's cost of acquisition into the affiliate's effective CAC.

name: Warm Process on Cold Leads Inversion
definition: Cold acquisition processes work on both cold and warm leads; warm-only processes fail on cold leads. The safe default is always the cold process because running warm leads through a cold process does not hurt conversion, but the reverse leaves cold leads unsold.

name: Soft Handoff Transition Model
definition: Instead of a 100% immediate transfer of clients to a new practitioner, the founder remains nominally in the loop (via shared notes, occasional messages, no live calls) while the new hire handles day-to-day delivery, reducing churn risk and theft risk during the transition.

name: Three Cash-Flow Pull-Forward Levers
definition: A three-part sequence for a capital-constrained product business to accelerate cash collection before acquiring a major asset: (1) renegotiate supplier payment terms to net 30/60/90, (2) use pre-orders to collect revenue before production, (3) maximize existing volume capacity to generate operating cash flow.

name: Direct Response Doom Loop
definition: A named cycle in paid-media e-commerce where revenue scales until CAC rises and gross margins compress until the business is a high-liability, low-margin operation that requires perpetual revenue just to sustain the team.

name: Brand Loop
definition: The virtuous cycle in which a strong brand promise attracts true believers (influencers, affiliates), the product reinforces that promise on delivery, and each satisfied customer deepens the brand rather than diluting it — enabling exits at premium multiples rather than product-arbitrage multiples.

name: Blue Mountain Paths Framework
definition: A decision model in which multiple distinct routes all reach the same destination (the prize); the mistake is spending time at the base choosing rather than climbing any of them.

name: Parallel Funnel Structure
definition: A mirror of the demand-generation funnel built for talent: app-gen, app-nurture, interviewing, onboarding, and retention/ascension for salespeople, running concurrently with the lead-gen funnel.

name: Uninformed Optimism to Breakthrough Arc
definition: A four-stage emotional arc in any hard endeavor: (1) uninformed optimism, (2) informed pessimism, (3) valley of despair (where most quit), (4) upside and goal achievement. Most people exit at stage 3 by starting a new cycle at stage 1.

name: Declarative vs Procedural Knowledge
definition: Two distinct knowledge types: declarative (knowing about — facts, concepts, how something works) versus procedural (knowing how — the embodied ability to execute). Declarative knowledge is commonly mistaken for procedural, causing false confidence.

name: Three Outcomes of Difficulty
definition: Every hard endeavor resolves in exactly one of three ways: (1) you quit, (2) external circumstances improve (it gets easier), or (3) you develop internally (you get harder). No fourth outcome exists; difficulty always ends.

name: Pick Your Price
definition: A three-stage offer structure: (1) market the offer as free, (2) at checkout present three donation/payment tiers (small/medium/large) with escalating bonuses, (3) end with a payment method question and silence. The framing attributes bonuses to staff, not the business, to exploit the consumer preference for paying employees over businesses.

name: C-L-O-S-E-R Formula
definition: A six-step in-person sales framework: Clarify why they are there, Label them with the problem, Overview their past pains, Sell the vacation (outcome), Explain away concerns, and Reinforce their decision.

name: Wedding Cake Gym Profit Model
definition: A three-tier revenue architecture (large-group training, semi-privates, supplements) where each tier carries both icing (front-end cash) and cake (recurring EFT), designed to accommodate the full customer buying curve and capture multiple spending wallets.

name: Gym Profit Levers
definition: Three macro levers that together transform gym profitability: Price Levers (membership price, billing cycle, membership levels), Capacity Levers (session duration, membership limits, attendance spread, physical space, exercise selection), and Overhead Levers (number of sessions, trainer hours, trainer pay).

name: Five Horsemen of Retention
definition: Five evidence-based retention tactics for membership businesses: Reach-Outs (personal touch every 14 days), Attendance Tracking (weekly red-flag identification), Handwritten Cards (at sign-up, referral requests, milestones), Member Events (every 21 days), and Exit Interviews (re-sell using C-L-O-S-E-R).

name: Client-Financed Acquisition
definition: A growth model in which a high-ticket front-end offer generates more cash on Day 1 than the cost of the leads that produced it, creating a negative acquisition cost and eliminating the need for a marketing budget.

name: Ideal Customer Life Cycle
definition: Four sequential customer milestones gym owners should deliberately engineer: Connect (form a relationship quickly), Refer/Testify (earn social proof and referrals), Testify/Refer (order may flip), and Ascend (upsell to higher-tier service or adjacent product).

name: Single-Highest-Leverage Activity Framework
definition: Identify the one activity that, if done alone, accomplishes all other objectives; hire people for everything else and concentrate founder time entirely on that activity.

name: Four Acquisition Channels
definition: Four non-referral channels for getting clients: paid ads, organic content, cold outreach, and affiliate/partner networks.

name: Sales God to Sales Training God Identity Shift
definition: A two-phase identity reframe for founder-dependent sales businesses: Phase 1 (sales god) — founder closes deals personally and maximizes individual output; Phase 2 (sales training god) — founder codifies their winning behaviors into teachable scripts and systems, and judges themselves by how well they can replicate their performance in others rather than by their own closing numbers.

name: Guaranteed Path + Fast Path Market Entry
definition: A two-track strategy for entering a new local market: one track is the reliable organic/outreach playbook (guaranteed path); the other is paid ads at low daily budget ($100-200/day) to accelerate learning (fast path). Both run concurrently.

name: CPA Channel Expansion Model
definition: Replace dependence on 3-4 high-value referral partners by expanding to 30-40 CPAs, converting each into an independent pipeline that collectively 10x lead flow without changing the core offer.

name: Sales Director Role Definition
definition: A sales director's primary function is culture maintenance and daily sales training — not closing deals or carrying specialized domain knowledge about the product being sold.

name: Scientific Attribution Model
definition: A hybrid attribution rule: if conversion happens within 30 days of a touchpoint, credit goes to last touch; if more than 30 days, credit goes to first touch. Designed to avoid over-indexing on direct response (last-touch only) or brand (first-touch only).

name: Delivery-Change Moral High Ground Pitch
definition: A three-part reframe for announcing a delivery reduction: (1) acknowledge you have been falling short of a stated promise, (2) position the change as restoring integrity rather than cutting service, (3) list reasons the new format benefits clients, making it socially costly for any client to object.

name: Big Head Long Tail Pricing
definition: Charge a large upfront fee for the primary value (skill or transformation), then price the continuity component much lower as a near-free network access fee, separating one-time value from consumable recurring value.

name: Five-Part Sales Training Rotation
definition: Break sales team training into five rotating daily segments: intro, discovery, offer, objections, looping, and a fifth wild-card slot for the current high-priority skill gap; rotate through all segments to keep every part of the script sharp.
concept: Daily Sales Role-Play as Training Cadence

name: Three-Bucket Avatar Segmentation
definition: Segment prospects by three independent axes: demographics (who they look like), quantifiables (business size and financials), and behaviors (actions correlated with activation and retention); only sell to avatars that score well on all three.

name: Management Diamond
definition: A diagnostic model for identifying why a person is not executing: they don't know they need to do it, don't know how, don't know when, have something blocking them, or lack motivation.

name: Inbound/Outbound Separation
definition: Inbound and outbound sales must be structurally separate teams; inbound is a reward earned by top closers, not a starting point for new salespeople.

name: Recruiting as Volume Game
definition: When talent is the binding constraint, the primary lever is 3-10x outreach volume and recruiter headcount, not recruiter quality alone — treated identically to a sales volume problem.

name: Talent Investment ROI Math
definition: A three-variable calculation (cost of hire, gross profit per hire, number of hires needed) that frames a recruiting budget as a capital-allocation decision with a stated payback multiple.

name: 20% Guaranteed Cost of Change
definition: Every change to a running business incurs a guaranteed ~20% performance decrement before any gains accrue; this floor means a change requires 50%+ upside to justify the certain downside.

name: Defend-the-Perimeter Scaling Metaphor
definition: A spatial analogy mapping business scope to physical defense area: the larger the territory a single person tries to defend, the less effective their defense becomes; a founder spreading across multiple business layers is analogous to guarding an entire city block solo.

name: Creative Agency Pivot: Volume Over Premium
definition: A two-pole strategic choice for AI ad production businesses: pursue true enterprise at $500K with the AI kept hidden, OR pursue mid-market clients spending $100K+/month in ads by replacing their entire creative team with high-volume AI-generated ads at a monthly retainer.

name: Four-Problem Upstream Diagnosis
definition: A four-part diagnostic for revenue-plateau businesses: avatar clarity, offer structure, positioning, and messaging — in that order of fix priority.

name: Map Clients to Product Suite (Not Product to Clients)
definition: Rather than customizing every offer to each client's venue, templatize a product suite (e.g., three core configurations) and map inbound clients to the nearest template.

name: Global vs. Local Positioning Frame
definition: For corporate or B2B buyers who will try to commoditize the offer, reframe the value conversation from line-item cost to total business cost (time, switching costs, headcount waste), so that price comparison becomes impossible.

name: Two-Path Exit Decision
definition: When a service business has both an operator-facing education or coaching arm and a corporate/studio arm, the founder must choose between path A (information/coaching = higher annual income, very hard to sell) and path B (corporate brand = lower annual income, easier to sell at exit); there is no viable third path.

name: Equity Decomposition Framework (Cash Flow, Sale, Risk, Control)
definition: A four-part breakdown of what equity actually consists of (cash flow, sale upside, risk, and control) used to identify which components an employee actually wants so non-dilutive alternatives can be offered in their place.

name: OTE Reverse-Engineering for Profit Share
definition: A compensation design process that starts from desired on-target earnings for a role, then back-calculates the profit-share percentage required to hit that OTE at expected profit levels, rather than picking an arbitrary percentage.

name: The Swamp
definition: A 1-3M revenue band where thin margins (10-20%) make hiring an operator impossible without absorbing the majority of available profit, trapping founders in a double-bind of either working two full-time roles or taking a large bet that destroys profitability.

name: Sales Motion Fix Before Pricing Fix
definition: A two-step repair sequence: fix the in-person close process first (order 1), then raise prices (order 2); reversing the sequence is ineffective because a broken sales motion causes the founder to attribute low conversion to price, when the real driver is process.

name: Direct Response Doom Loop
definition: A compressing cycle in which e-commerce revenue scales via paid media while margins simultaneously shrink until the business becomes a high-liability, low-margin operation that must keep selling just to sustain its cost base.

name: Eugene Schwartz Awareness Pyramid
definition: A five-level customer awareness hierarchy (Unaware, Problem Aware, Solution Aware, Product Aware, Most Aware) that determines what hook and click journey a prospect requires; bridge pages convert colder audiences by stepping them up through levels before reaching a sales page.

name: Brand Completion Loop
definition: A virtuous cycle in which a strong brand promise attracts customers, the product experience either reinforces or destroys that promise, and reinforcement deepens brand equity, enabling premium pricing and affiliate recruitment.

name: Marketing as Filtration System
definition: Marketing finds ideal buyers who already exist rather than educating or converting beginners into ideal buyers; the message and medium select for the avatar, not transform them.

name: Nail It Then Scale It
definition: A sequencing rule for brick-and-mortar expansion: prove that a single unit operates at sustained performance without the founder before committing capital to a second location.

name: Founder-Absence Test (Three-to-Six Month)
definition: A three-to-six month period in which the founder fully withdraws from day-to-day operations to confirm the GM can sustain or grow the business independently; failure during this window blocks expansion.

name: ACQ Universe Framework
definition: A layered monetization architecture: free media generates attention, low-ticket conversions (books, email list, School) capture contact info, a tiered advisory practice converts business owners to high-ticket, and excess capital redeploys into real estate, ventures, and private equity — all anchored by one brand credibility promise.

name: Disney Analogy for B2B Media Company
definition: Maps Acquisition.com revenue streams to Disney properties: brand = Disney Studios, ACQ Network = Disney+, Advisory Practice = theme parks, School = Star Wars/Marvel franchise investments, insurance/sales AI/lending = future spokes — illustrating how a single brand supports multiple distinct monetization arms.

name: One-Level-Up Channel Strategy
definition: Instead of acquiring end customers directly, acquire the intermediary (agency, guru, mentor) who already has those customers, capturing higher LTV via onboarding fees, per-customer fees, and recurring top-line percentage.

name: Choose Your Regrets Framework
definition: Christopher Hitchens' principle that because opportunity cost is structurally unavoidable in all human choices, regrets are inevitable rather than the result of bad decisions; the only agency available is choosing which regrets to accept.

name: Close-Rate Pricing Gap Framework
definition: A five-tier scale mapping close-rate ranges to the implied pricing multiple still available; 80%+ close rate = 3-4x multiple remaining; 30-40% = appropriately priced; below 30% = execution problem.

name: Service Pricing Virtuous Cycle
definition: A closed loop in which higher price enables higher gross margins, which fund better talent, which deliver better service, which improve reputation, which drive demand, which justify higher price.

name: Learning to Try Framework
definition: A meta-skill built by committing all discretionary effort, money, and time to one domain for an extended daily period; once internalized, the skill of sustained commitment transfers across domains (saxophone, tennis, content creation) because skill acquisition follows the same pattern everywhere.

name: Tolerance as First Component of Mental Toughness
definition: A model of mental toughness whose first component, tolerance, measures how long a person maintains their intended behavior before hardship causes a behavioral change (the fuse length), distinct from pain tolerance or pain ignorance.

name: Felix Dennis Eleven Levels of Wealth
definition: Felix Dennis's framework from his book on wealth breaks affluence into 11 tiers, asserting that only at $400M+ liquid does a person achieve true financial freedom where they can do whatever they want without constraint.
concept: Wealth Tiers and Diminishing Marginal Returns

name: Four-Strategy Compounding Framework
definition: Alex Hormozi's four-part personal finance strategy: (1) increase income, (2) reduce spending, (3) invest faster/earlier, (4) invest in income-earning skills as the highest-ROI lever.

name: Skill Bridge Metaphor
definition: Skills are individual bridge segments; missing links prevent income from crossing to the other side. Each purchase adds a segment, and no single skill alone spans the gap — the full chain compounds.

name: Learning Budget Model
definition: Allocate a fixed monthly percentage of income (1–10%) as a mandatory spending budget for skill experiments and education; the mechanism is forced experimentation, not optional learning.

name: Do Epic Then Talk Framework
definition: Two-step sequence for building credibility and providing value: (1) achieve something notable or accumulate effort-proof, then (2) talk about what you learned from it.

name: Behavioral Change Intensity Scale
definition: A two-pole axis measuring how much behavior changes after a person's tolerance threshold is exceeded: high fortitude = minimal behavior change; low fortitude = severe, life-disrupting behavior change.

name: Six Principles for Building Capacity Before Knowing What to Do
definition: A six-part framework for ambitious people who lack direction: (1) build physical and financial capacity, (2) save money to buy optionality, (3) add and practice skills, (4) build an audience without a product, (5) build a waitlist before building the thing, (6) expand luck surface area via networking and geographic proximity.

name: Skill Stacking Multiplier
definition: Each new skill acquired multiplies the value of all previously held skills; the portfolio grows super-linearly because each layer has the prior layers as prerequisites.

name: Conscious Trade Accounting Framework
definition: Every life outcome requires a conscious trade of something valued (time, safety, experience); the framework asks two questions: what am I trading, and what am I trading it for.

name: Upside-Downside Asymmetry Frame
definition: Downside is bounded (survivable worst-case) while upside is unbounded; therefore rational action tilts toward taking bets rather than avoiding them.

name: RFP Frame + Value Frame Stack
definition: Every B2B sales conversation operates on two simultaneous layers: the RFP frame (commodity, price-comparison, minimum viable compliance) and the value frame (holistic business impact, ROI, total cost of risk). Winning requires satisfying the RFP floor while differentiating on the value ceiling.

name: Case Study Close
definition: In procurement-driven (RFP) sales contexts, attach a peer-company case study to every bid that demonstrates the most analogous client's outcome on the exact value drivers (speed, risk reduction, total cost) the buyer cares about; the prior client's voice displaces price-only comparison.

name: Flip to Motivated Buyer Framework
definition: When sellers are structurally reluctant (frozen market), redirect sourcing effort toward buyers with forced timelines (e.g., 1031 exchange deadlines) who must transact regardless of market conditions, and use their urgency to extract seller-side deals.

name: Community-as-CRM Asset Framework
definition: Build a gated community of your exact target avatar (e.g., multifamily owners in one metro), position yourself as the gating function, and over time create a proprietary CRM that is both an ongoing deal-flow engine and a sellable business asset.

name: Commission-to-Consulting Revenue Conversion
definition: Replace variable transaction-based commission income with a fixed annual consulting retainer; subsidize the retainer with a deeply discounted commission rate so the recurring income is pulled forward while clients perceive total cost as lower.

name: Conviction-Trust Sales Framework
definition: Two necessary conditions for any sale: (1) the seller must have near-total conviction in their product, and (2) the prospect must develop enough trust in the seller that the seller's conviction transfers to the prospect.

name: Consultative Sales Sequence
definition: A structured conversation sequence: (1) why are they there / what is the problem, (2) what have they tried and failed, (3) why your product is different from what failed, (4) ask permission to explain the product, (5) explain through experiences (not features), using analogies, (6) close.

name: No-Based Close
definition: Framing the close as a soft opt-out question (e.g., 'Would you mind?') rather than a hard yes-demand, exploiting natural communication dynamics where 'I don't mind' is easier to say than 'yes'.

name: Content-First to Paid Ads Sequencing
definition: A three-step acquisition sequence: (1) post organic before/after content, (2) identify the best-performing piece, (3) attach a CTA and run it as a paid ad.

name: Leaky Bucket Retention Diagnosis
definition: Track monthly joins versus cancels to classify a recurring-revenue business as growing, flat, or declining; MRR grows only when new members exceed churned members.

name: Day-90 / Month-6 Retention Milestones
definition: Two tactical milestones that predict long-term retention: surviving day 90 cuts monthly churn roughly in half; surviving month 6 cuts it by ~5x again, because churn drops from 20%+ in months 1-3 to ~10% at day 90, then to ~2% by month 6.

name: Snakes and Ladders Onboarding
definition: Remove snakes (friction, confusion, overwhelm) and front-load ladders (the stickiest value moments) in onboarding; new members should hit a fast win within the first 24 hours.

name: Lead-with-Free Client Acquisition Framework
definition: A three-stage approach for zero-track-record service founders: (1) offer free engagement (e.g., five calls) to eliminate the proof barrier, (2) win on service quality and aligned incentive signaling, (3) convert to paid or collect testimonial for future paid prospects.

name: Four Business Shapes
definition: All businesses belong to one of four structural types (e-commerce, service, education/info-media, SaaS) each with a characteristic growth curve, dominant scaling constraint, and competitive moat requirement.

name: Big Head, Long Tail
definition: In education businesses, charge a high one-time price for the core knowledge (big head) and a much lower recurring price for consumable ongoing elements such as community, updated resources, or continued education (long tail); the mismatch in value is what drives churn when the ratio is reversed.

name: Attraction-Conversion-Delivery
definition: Every business has three structural layers: attraction (getting leads), conversion (closing sales), and delivery (fulfilling the service or product); the layer that requires human labor is the scaling constraint.

name: Network-vs-Headhunter Role Routing
definition: Hire within your own network for roles where you have domain expertise to evaluate candidates; use a paid headhunter for roles where you lack that expertise (e.g., finance, tech, HR at leadership level).

name: Cringe-as-Status-Play Reframe
definition: A three-part disambiguation of cringe, shame, and guilt that exposes cringe as a defensive status signal from the observer, not evidence of wrongdoing by the actor.
concept: Shame vs. Guilt: Navigating Social Signals

name: Seven-Step Outlearning Process
definition: A sequential learning framework: (1) define learning as same-condition behavior change, (2) deconstruct the skill into sub-behaviors, (3) define measurable success criteria, (4) ignore causal explanations and focus on observable inputs/outputs, (5) model top performers by observing what they do, (6) analyze differences between your top 10% attempts and the rest and iterate, (7) repeat with increasing volume.

name: Skill Deconstruction Hierarchy
definition: Skills are nested from broad domain labels (business, basketball) down through sub-skills (marketing, dribbling) to atomic observable behaviors that can be tracked and changed; learning requires chunking down to the observable behavioral unit.

name: Four Investor Acquisition Channels
definition: Four discrete paths to reach accredited investors without relying on a personal network: (1) affiliate model (leverage someone else's existing investor audience), (2) paid ads (targeted to accredited investors), (3) content marketing (thought leadership that attracts inbound investor interest), (4) outbound direct contact.

name: Three Levels of Salesmanship
definition: A progression from solo selling (Level 1) to recruiting individual salespeople (Level 2) to building and leading a team of salespeople (Level 3); each level requires a distinct identity and skill set.

name: Downside Tolerance Decision Filter
definition: A four-question sequence for evaluating any new opportunity: (1) What is the upside? (2) What is the downside? (3) Can I live with the downside? (4) How likely is the downside? If the downside is not livable, do not proceed regardless of upside.

name: Licensing Approval Gate
definition: A single prerequisite criterion before permitting expansion into a licensing model: the existing core business must be able to open a new location every quarter using a team that does not include the founder, with zero founder involvement required.

name: Ham and Garlic Framework
definition: Wrap the thing customers need (garlic: continuity) inside the thing they want (ham: a defined short program); the front-end defined offer is a sales vehicle, not the end product.

name: Two-Pronged Distribution Layer Strategy
definition: When building a distribution layer, combine (1) a launch campaign for immediate value for affiliates/distributors who have no initial trust, and (2) a long-term retention journey that treats distributors as customers with their own customer journey.

name: Observable Universe Definitions Framework
definition: Define every abstract concept in terms of observable, court-admissible behaviors so that all parties share a common referent and communication becomes precise rather than amorphous.

name: Behavior-Ladder Decomposition
definition: Break abstract trait labels (confident, lazy, patient) into the discrete observable micro-behaviors that cause observers to apply those labels, then address those micro-behaviors directly.

name: Traffic-Volume Funnel Routing
definition: Route cold traffic to live call (small volume) or webinar with post-webinar 1-on-1 solicitation (larger volume); funnel structure scales with traffic quantity.

name: Unit Economics as North Star for Franchising
definition: Every franchise-demand problem traces back to a single root cause: the unit economics of each individual restaurant. Fix per-location profitability first; franchise interest is a downstream symptom.

name: Four-Part Profit Optimization Sequence
definition: A sequential four-step plan to triple EBITDA without growing revenue: (1) fix the sales motion, (2) optimize pricing and packaging per service line, (3) generate sufficient cash flow, (4) acquire the talent needed to shore up infrastructure.

name: Cloud-to-Dirt Knowledge Stack
definition: Vertical integration of knowledge from high-level strategy and people management down to granular API-level technical execution; enables a business owner to see cross-functional AI applications that pure technologists cannot.

name: AI as Tool, Not Business
definition: Businesses should use AI as one of many operational tools (analogous to the internet) rather than repositioning themselves as AI companies; the goal is better outcomes, not AI as identity.

name: Barbell AI Strategy
definition: Position on two extremes simultaneously: high-risk AI-first operations on one pole, and bets on timeless human needs (health, food, entertainment) on the other; avoid the unprotected middle.

name: Workflow-First Org Design
definition: Replace role-based org thinking with workflow-based thinking: decompose every role into four to ten granular tasks, then ask which tasks can live inside an automated workflow instead of headcount.

name: BYOS / BYOA Model
definition: Bring Your Own Software or Bring Your Own Agent: employees and contractors who arrive with pre-built AI agents that replace entire department outputs become disproportionately valuable as labor markets shift.

name: Five-Stage Script Rotation
definition: A weekly cycle of daily role-play sessions covering five discrete script stages: intro, discovery, offer, objections/looping, and a rotating hot-topic day based on team need.
concept: Daily Sales Role-Play as Training Cadence

name: Three-Bucket Avatar Segmentation
definition: Segment prospects into three classification buckets before selling: demographics (who they look like), quantifiables (business size, revenue), and behaviors (actions taken by activated vs. churned look-alikes).

name: Uncertainty and Delay as Upside Filter
definition: All the upside worth having in life sits behind exactly two prices people refuse to pay: uncertainty (not knowing the outcome) and delay (not knowing how long to wait). Because most people are unwilling to bear these two costs, they remain the primary arbitrage opportunities.

name: Trial-to-Activation Rebate Structure
definition: Two-step activation incentive: charge upfront, then rebate or waive the next billing period if the user completes a defined first-value action within the trial window.

name: 40-for-2 Management Trade
definition: A founder trades 40 hours of direct selling for 2 hours of managing a salesperson; repeating this trade accumulates until the founder does 40 hours of management and zero selling, then can trade management hours to a manager in turn.

name: Free Trial Plus Penalty Model
definition: A client-acquisition model where prospects receive service at no charge in exchange for behaving as required; failure to complete specified behaviors triggers a billing event. Designed to fill a proof pipeline when testimonials are absent.

name: Two End-States Framework for Client-Acquisition Agencies
definition: Client-acquisition agencies for small businesses inevitably converge on one of two viable long-run business models: ultra-low-cost high-volume automation or high-ticket whale/upmarket positioning. The mid-market is structurally unviable.

name: Click-to-Close Vertical Integration
definition: The strategic goal of owning every stage of the deal pipeline from lead sourcing through close, eliminating external gatekeepers and capturing full margin at each stage.
concept: Vertical Integration to Capture Full Margin

name: Defend the Perimeter Metaphor
definition: A spatial analogy comparing business focus to a single soldier guarding progressively larger areas: one room (easy), one building (hard), one block (nearly impossible) — illustrating that spreading attention across multiple business lines is like a single defender guarding an entire city block alone.

name: Moral High Ground Delivery Change Pitch
definition: A reframing technique for announcing service delivery reductions: position the change as fulfilling a prior promise (integrity), enumerate reasons it benefits clients, and make any objection equivalent to attacking your integrity.

name: Want Less or Trade More
definition: A two-option resolution to the dissatisfaction of wanting two incompatible things: either reduce your desire for the thing you say you want, or pay the cost required to get it. There is no third path.

name: Active Active, Passive Passive
definition: A capital management rule: keep passive investments managed by partners so they consume no founder attention, and deploy active effort only in the core business where ROI on time is highest.

name: Three Value-Creation Frames for Premium Offers
definition: Three intellectual attack vectors for designing a premium one-on-one offer: (1) 10x/100x price thought-experiment to identify low-cost high-value inclusions; (2) word-of-mouth-only constraint to identify what a perfect customer experience requires; (3) remove-unscalable-but-keep-10x-value to engineer a scalable high-value tier.

name: Premium Tier Anchor Model
definition: A two-tier offer structure where an expensive unscalable one-on-one tier is listed alongside a scalable lower-priced tier; the premium tier anchors perceived value for the scalable tier via price contrast and brand narrative even if few or no premium buyers convert.

name: SPCL Influence Framework
definition: Four stacked components that determine a creator's influence over an audience: Status (controls scarce resources), Power (say-do correspondence reinforcement history), Credibility (third-party verifiable proof), Likeness (demographic or values similarity).

name: Proof-Promise-Plan Introduction Structure
definition: A three-part educational video introduction: Proof (demonstrate you can do what you claim), Promise (state what the viewer will get or learn), Plan (set expectations for what happens next). Order is flexible; lead with whichever element matters most for the specific content.

name: Four Audience Monetization Paths
definition: Four ways to monetize an existing audience arranged on a continuum from low-risk/low-value to high-risk/high-value: (1) Affiliates — paid after sales on standard terms; (2) Sponsorships — paid upfront as a traffic source via CPM or whitelisting; (3) Partnerships — equity deals ranging from minority to majority; (4) Own brand — start your own product from scratch.

name: Bouquet Brand Assembly Model
definition: A brand is built by deliberately pairing your product or name with people, experiences, and values your ideal customer already likes. Each pairing is a flower; repeated positive pairings form a bouquet (the brand). Narrowing means clustering similar flowers; expanding means adding adjacent ones. A single bad pairing is one rotten flower that mars the whole bouquet.

name: Six-Step First $100K Roadmap
definition: A sequential six-step plan to earn and bank $100K: (1) cut all costs, (2) save time, (3) research skills people already pay for, (4) spend time learning through iteration, (5) spend money on tools/implementation/attempts, (6) do not increase lifestyle.

name: 4-4-4 Time Split
definition: For people without a 9-to-5 job, divide 12 active hours into three four-hour blocks: promotion (letting people know about your offer), delivery (fulfilling what you promised), and building (researching opportunities and prioritizing next actions).

name: First-Party Data Learning Loop
definition: A five-step skill-acquisition cycle: (1) do high volume, (2) analyze the top 10% of outputs, (3) identify what differentiates the top 10% from the bottom 90%, (4) isolate the most important variable, (5) avoid the mistakes the bottom 90% keeps making, then repeat 100 more times.

name: 1-1-1 Rule
definition: Sell one product or service to one avatar on one channel until you reach $1 million in revenue; do not diversify until that milestone is hit.

name: Trim and Stack Offer Design
definition: A two-phase offer redesign process: first list all current deliverables, then cut and recombine them to retain only what clients most value, eliminating everything else to reduce delivery load while maintaining perceived value.

name: Supply Constraint Resolution Matrix
definition: Four-lever framework for resolving capacity bottlenecks in service businesses: (1) raise price so fewer people buy, (2) change delivery ratio from 1:1 to 1:N, (3) productize the delivery, or (4) hire additional staff to absorb load.

name: Systemic vs. Segment Market Diagnosis
definition: Before committing to a business model pivot, determine whether the revenue decline is systemic (affecting all carriers/markets nationwide) or segment-specific (one state or carrier class); only a systemic verdict justifies model-level change.

name: Back-End Continuity Test
definition: Before replacing a transactional model with a subscription model, first test whether existing emergency/transactional customers can be converted to a recurring offer at point of service completion; inability to close that upsell is decisive evidence against switching the entire model.

name: Passion Etymology Reframe
definition: Reframes passion from doing what you love to finding something meaningful enough to suffer for, rooted in the Latin root passio (suffering) and its first usage in the Passion of Christ.

name: Fixed-Cost Suffering Model
definition: All life paths (employment, entrepreneurship, wealth, poverty, marriage, singlehood) carry equivalent suffering; the variable is what you receive in return, not whether you suffer.
concept: All Life Paths Have Equal Suffering; Outcome Differs by Choice

name: Return on Capital Optimization
definition: Evaluate every business model variant by return on human capital and financial capital, not by absolute revenue or profit, to identify which format to scale.

name: Split-the-Difference Roll-Up Offer
definition: A two-stage multiple structure for DSO consolidation: offer practice owners their solo-sale multiple (4x) plus a share of the uplift to the consolidated multiple (7x), keeping everything above 7x as the consolidator's profit.

name: Coaching Business as the Goose
definition: Retain the educational/coaching business as the permanent demand-generation asset (the goose) while selling the consolidated practice group (the eggs); the coaching book supplies a continual pipeline of willing consolidation targets.

name: Logic-Evidence-Utility Problem Definition
definition: A three-question framework for defining business problems: What does it mean? How do you know that? Why do you care? Forces quantitative grounding of vague claims.

name: Fast Cash Play via Existing Resources
definition: A structured approach to generating immediate client revenue by mobilizing the client's existing dead contacts, old customers, or social media audience before new acquisition begins, making the product self-funding within 30 days.

name: Win Your Money Back Dual-Conversion Frame
definition: A $500 front-end offer where the customer earns a 100% store credit by completing daily habits (activation) and submitting multi-platform testimonials (referral), with the store credit rolling into a full-year upsell at $5,000; combines results guarantee with social proof harvest in one mechanism.

name: Court Filter for Observable Language
definition: A translation discipline requiring all marketing and sales copy to describe only observable, court-admissible behaviors — physical actions, visible states, measurable outputs — removing interpretive or amorphous language that cannot be verified by a third party.

name: Four Components of Equity Framework
definition: A decomposition of what real equity provides into four distinct elements — cash flow, sale bonus, risk, and control — used to identify which elements a prospective equity recipient actually wants, then substitute non-dilutive instruments for those elements only.

name: Profit Share Pool Structure
definition: A fixed percentage of annual profit (typically 10–20%) set aside as a leadership incentive pool; individual leaders receive slices of that pool rather than ownership stakes, capping total incentive spend while creating owner-like thinking.

name: Scientific Attribution Model
definition: A blended attribution rule: if a conversion occurs within 30 days of a touchpoint, assign credit to the last touch; if the gap exceeds 30 days, assign credit to the first touch. This prevents both the short-termism of pure last-touch and the brand-inflation of pure first-touch.

name: Sales Hire Pattern Recognition Ladder
definition: Founders must fail-and-learn through each successive sales hire type (SDR, salesperson, manager, closer, director, VP) in sequence; pattern recognition for what good looks like only comes after multiple failures at each level.

name: Investor Risk Ladder
definition: Investors pay down assumptions, not ideas; each funding round represents a specific assumption that was de-risked, and the valuation step-up must be justified by naming which assumption was proved true.

name: Find-Sell-Deliver Sequence
definition: When launching a new product or service, identify a customer first, sell them on a meaningful outcome you will deliver, and commit to staying until they are satisfied — before building a detailed plan or process.

name: Evidence-Over-Emotion Self-Assessment
definition: Apply the same evidence standard you use to judge others when evaluating your own character traits; a trait is possessed if observable behavior demonstrates it, regardless of whether you feel you have it.

name: Penny-on-the-Scale Accumulation
definition: Build a character trait by accumulating undeniable behavioral proof one action at a time; each act consistent with the trait adds a penny to the scale until the weight of evidence makes the self-attribution credible.

name: Three-Lever Scaling Sequence
definition: Fix sales motion first, then raise prices, then use increased profit to improve talent retention — each lever unlocks the next.

name: Expanded Career Ladder Framework
definition: Transform a 3-rung career path into 9-18 rungs by subdividing each existing title into junior, normal, and senior tiers, alternating title upgrades with tangible perks so employees are always 3-6 months from the next promotion.

name: Debt Risk Quantification Framework
definition: A three-variable coverage-ratio analysis (seasonal volatility, cash flow vs. debt covenants, and coverage buffer) that converts emotional debt fear into a tractable math problem.

name: Partnership Justification Test
definition: A partnership only creates value if the partner supplies something the other party genuinely lacks: money, time, skills, or network/distribution. Absence of at least one gap means one partner is redundant.

name: Drop Low or Go High
definition: Digital marketing agencies serving SMBs must choose between two viable poles: ultra-cheap automated delivery (≤$400/month) or high-touch services for sophisticated business owners with proven metrics; the middle band ($1,500–$3,000/month) is a structural dead zone.

name: Launch and Integrate Affiliate Strategy
definition: Two-step process for activating affiliate partners: a launch (get a batch of referrals fast to create an internal case study) followed by integration (embedding your service into the affiliate's standard onboarding so every new client they bring on automatically becomes your client).

name: Three-Level AI Service Delivery Stack
definition: A progression from websites (level 1) to individual automations (level 2) to full integrated AI systems (level 3), where each level requires more skill and commands higher prices.

name: Doctor Sales Approach
definition: A consultative sales structure where the seller talks 20% of the time and asks diagnostic questions that lead the prospect to identify their own pain, then positions the seller's service as the solution.

name: Five-Step AI Business Roadmap
definition: A sequenced roadmap from (1) learn a skill, (2) work for free to build proof, (3) sustain yourself ($2-3k/month), (4) charge more and filter clients, to (5) identify and solve bottlenecks continuously.

name: Result-Based vs Upfront-Plus-Recurring Pricing
definition: Two primary pricing structures for AI services: (1) result-based at 20% of gross profit generated, which pays more when work is excellent but requires trackability; (2) upfront setup fee plus monthly recurring, used when results are hard to attribute, combined with a satisfaction guarantee.

name: Face-Saving Re-Entry Frame
definition: A negotiation tactic for allowing a previously resistant partner to accept new terms without humiliation by reframing their earlier refusal as contextually reasonable and shifting blame to external demand pressure.
concept: Partnership Equity Must Be Codified Upfront

name: Replace-Six Strategy
definition: Rather than scaling headcount of low-quality clients, replace current clients one-for-one with higher-quality, compliant clients over a 12-month horizon, then go deep on the single best performer.

name: Sell to the Rich, Not From Your Own Wallet
definition: A pricing mindset framework asserting that entrepreneurs should price for the top 10% wealth tier (net worth $1M+), not for the 50th-percentile buyer who is never their customer.

name: LTV-Conversion Rate Model Selection Test
definition: Compare two business models by asking three questions: (1) Does more of the target market want it (conversion rate)? (2) Is the lifetime gross profit per customer higher? (3) Is the CAC comparable or lower? The model that wins on all three is mathematically superior.

name: Build for Cold, Works Everywhere
definition: A sales process designed for cold (unwarmed) leads will function on warm leads too; a warm-only process underconverts cold traffic by a large margin.
concept: Warm vs. Cold Sales Process Mismatch

name: Cold-Lead Sales Architecture (VSL + Qualification + Training Cadence)
definition: Three-component system for cold traffic: (1) video sales letter pre-frames and filters before the call, (2) structured qualification gates separate genuinely qualified prospects, (3) ongoing sales training cadence ensures reps follow the process consistently.

name: Today Constraint vs. Horizon Constraint
definition: Distinguish the constraint blocking growth right now (sales/marketing) from the constraint that will become existential at a future date (facility/supply); address today's constraint first unless the horizon constraint's timeline forces earlier action.

name: Launch Then Integrate B2B Partnership Model
definition: Enter a B2B partner relationship with a one-time co-webinar launch to build goodwill, then transition to permanent integration into the partner's sales motion so the partner sells the complementary service as a standard add-on.

name: Three Content Buckets
definition: A taxonomy dividing all content into entertainment (goal: get people to watch), education (goal: change behavior), and edutainment (goal: both simultaneously); the prevailing outcome across viewers determines the bucket.

name: Proof-Promise-Plan Introduction Framework
definition: A three-element intro structure for educational videos: Proof (evidence you can deliver on the promise), Promise (what the viewer will gain), and Plan (the steps that will get them there); order is flexible — lead with whichever element is most important for the content.

name: Four Paths to Mega Money
definition: A 2x2 framework combining your money vs. other people's money with your business vs. other people's businesses to produce four distinct wealth-building paths: bootstrapping, raising capital, investing, and fund management.

name: The Going Pro Decision
definition: A binary internal commitment to pursue a skill or goal at a professional standard, triggered by a deliberate choice rather than external circumstances, with the understanding that effort will never receive credit and that the work must be done purely for the doer.

name: Six Levels of Making Money
definition: A ranked hierarchy of six compensation structures ordered by leverage and risk transfer: (1) work then get paid, (2) pay as you go, (3) pay then work, (4) when X happens you pay me, (5) sell/buy risk itself (insurance model), (6) no-matter-what you pay me (government/monopoly model).

name: Behavioral Trauma Framework
definition: A two-axis diagnostic: (1) was there a permanent behavior change from an aversive stimulus? — determines whether trauma occurred; (2) does the behavior change move toward or away from long-term goals? — determines whether the outcome was positive or negative.

name: Performance-Based Zero-Capital Business Selection
definition: Two criteria for selecting a business when starting with zero capital: (1) pay-for-performance compensation structure so no capital is required to start earning, and (2) no upfront cost to operate, meaning the business runs on the founder's time and skill rather than inventory or ad spend.

name: Vendor Discount Arbitrage Model
definition: Negotiate a discounted bulk rate from a business owner (the delivery cost floor), then sell to end customers at a higher market price; the spread between the two prices is the founder's margin, requiring zero capital if the business owner absorbs delivery cost.

name: Scale Zero Framework
definition: A principle requiring the founder to reduce their personal operational contribution to zero before a business can scale without ceiling; any function requiring founder involvement caps total scale.

name: Two Service Business Reinvestment Paths
definition: Service businesses should direct end-of-year profit into exactly two categories: A-player talent acquisition or brand-building plays (high-visibility associations), not physical capital or general overhead.

name: Five AI Workers System
definition: A bundled agency offer of five AI-driven automation roles — outreach specialist (database reactivation), reputation manager (reviews and referrals), website lead nurturing, AI receptionist (missed call text-back), and marketing director (paid ads with AI lead nurturing) — sold together to local businesses as an inseparable department rather than individual point solutions.

name: Niche Research Rubric
definition: A four-criteria filter for selecting an agency niche: proof of concept (other agencies succeeding), total addressable market over 10,000 businesses, high-ticket recurring pricing, and a growing market; evaluated via Claude prompt.
concept: Market Selection Criteria: Growth + Pain + Targeting

name: The Lonely Chapter
definition: The early phase of an exceptional path during which the founder operates without peer support, social validation, or external cheering — positioned as a diagnostic signal that you are on the right path rather than a problem to solve.

name: Protect Your Passion
definition: A discipline of actively preserving enthusiasm for one's work during the low-reward early phase, because passion destroyed before compounding kicks in inverts the effort-reward relationship at precisely the time when effort has the highest return.

name: Epic Story Razor
definition: A personal decision heuristic: when choosing between paths, default to whichever option produces the most compelling long-arc narrative, on the reasoning that the most epic story is almost never the shortest path to victory.

name: Close Rate to Price Multiple Ladder
definition: A tiered rule-of-thumb mapping sales close rates to implied pricing multiples, used to diagnose whether a business is underpriced relative to demand.

name: LTV:CAC Human-in-Loop Multiplier
definition: A framework for setting minimum LTV-to-CAC ratio targets based on how many of three business functions (attraction, conversion, delivery) involve a human, scaling from 3:1 (all automated) to 12:1 (all human).

name: 30-Day Cash Collected Rule
definition: A cash-flow rule requiring that gross profit collected within the first 30 days of a new customer relationship exceeds the sum of CAC plus COGS, enabling a self-funding acquisition loop.

name: Creator Risk Continuum
definition: A spectrum from low-risk to high-risk creator types (entertainer -> B2C educator -> B2B prosumer -> B2B expert) where risk is defined by the potential downside to the consumer of acting on the advice; AI disrupts low-risk types first and high-risk types last.

name: Self-Linking Business Loop
definition: An engineered business motion in which the delivery of the core product or service generates marketing proof automatically, which in turn acquires more customers, creating a compounding self-reinforcing cycle.

name: Discount Plus One-Time Fee Structure
definition: A two-part offer that charges a deeply discounted first term to lower acquisition friction, then adds one or more separately named one-time fees to fund commissions and reduce churn.

name: Four-Step One-Time Fee Creation
definition: A four-step process to create any made-up fee: (1) pick fee name, (2) pick fee price, (3) pick reason why, (4) start charging, discounting, or waiving it.

name: Pareto Pricing Tier Framework
definition: Four price tiers with 5-10x gaps between each level; expect approximately 20% of customers at each tier to take the next step up; top tiers generate the majority of profit despite minority of customers.
concept: Pareto Pricing: Four-Tier Structure at 5-10x Gaps

name: Replace-Not-Scale Partner Sequencing
definition: When a partner portfolio has low-quality or legally gray clients, the path to growth is replacing the six existing clients with six higher-quality ones before attempting volume expansion.

name: R&D Distribution Model
definition: A monthly R&D process where the operator selects 10-20 representative accounts from the distribution base, assigns a fixed budget to test one pressing problem, and releases results to the full base monthly regardless of outcome.

name: Must Be Easy Diagnostic
definition: The phrase 'it must be easy' is a reliable surface signal of victim-mindset; its presence indicates the speaker is externalizing inadequacy onto circumstances rather than accepting agency.

name: Three Macro Opportunity Waves
definition: A sequence of three platform-level macro opportunities — Internet, Web 2.0 (social), and AI — each spawning sub-layer micro opportunities (e.g., Google Search, Facebook Ads, TikTok Shop) with outsized early-mover returns before market stabilization.

name: Blended ROI Channel Evaluation
definition: Evaluating marketing channels by blended return rather than transaction-proximity ROI: a channel ranks high when it captures new attention AND nurtures existing audience, not merely when it sits closest to purchase.

name: Shorts as Ads to Longs
definition: Short-form content functions as top-of-funnel advertising whose goal is to drive audiences into longer-form content with greater total time-on-brand, not to serve as the primary engagement vehicle.

name: Supply-First Growth Sequence
definition: A four-to-five step prioritization sequence for operationally constrained service businesses: (1) fix supply constraint via offshoring for 2-3x delivery leverage, (2) add data architecture layer, (3) add AI layer on top of data, (4) re-open sales once new capacity is proven, (5) use existing marketing assets to grow demand.

name: Reverse-Engineer Your Enjoyment
definition: A two-step creator sustainability framework: (1) identify which content formats you genuinely enjoy making, (2) capture yourself doing activities you already enjoy — then build your publishing schedule around that intersection rather than around algorithmic or audience expectations.

name: One True Lever Test
definition: A focus-finding exercise: ask which single thing, if true in one year, would 10x the company and make all other goals follow automatically.

name: Database-First Reactivation Sequence
definition: Before running any paid ads, re-engage existing lead databases and active members for reviews and referrals; use those results to build trust and organically improve ad performance before investing in cold marketing.
concept: Fast Cash Plays via Underutilized Assets

name: Five-Point AI Agency Pitch Structure
definition: A five-part done-for-you offer pitched sequentially: (1) database reactivation via AI, (2) review and referral automation for members, (3) targeted paid ad marketing with landing pages, (4) AI-powered lead nurture and follow-up, (5) inbound call tracking and AI answering to capture missed organic prospects.

name: Clarity-First Sales Positioning
definition: Pitch only one or two simple, clear outcomes on the first call to minimize decision complexity; upsell additional services only after delivering initial results and building trust, because clarity drives faster purchase decisions.

name: Drop Low or Go High Framework
definition: For client-acquisition agencies serving SMBs, the only viable business models are ultra-cheap high-automation or high-touch premium; the mid-market is a dead zone where margin compresses to zero.

name: Supply-First Scaling Sequence
definition: When supply (capacity) is the binding constraint, the operational fix order is: (1) fix pricing/packaging to free cash flow, (2) deploy cash into paid ads with attribution, (3) invest freed margin into content/thought leadership.

name: Talent Funnel as Parallel Demand Funnel
definition: A sales organization must build a mirror talent-acquisition funnel (applicant generation, nurture, interviewing, onboarding, retention/ascension) that receives the same budget discipline as the customer demand funnel.

name: SPCL Influence Framework
definition: Four independent but combinable components of influence: Status (control of reinforcers), Power (say-do correspondence), Credibility (third-party proof), and Likeness (similarity to audience).

name: Management Diamond
definition: A five-cause diagnostic for why an employee (or entrepreneur) is not executing a task: they did not know the task was required, did not know how to do it, did not know the deadline, had a blocking constraint, or did not want to do it — in that order of actual frequency.
concept: The Execution Gap: Knowledge Without Action

name: Declarative vs. Procedural Content Split
definition: Free content teaches declarative knowledge (about how something works — what you use to sell); paid products deliver procedural knowledge (how to execute — what you sell). Mixing them causes either free riders or stalled purchases.

name: Choke Point Pre-Mortem
definition: Before a high-stakes live event, enumerate every discrete failure mode in priority order and build a pre-assigned fallback for each one before the event begins.

name: $100M Series Stacking Architecture
definition: A three-book product series structured so each book layers one additional skill on top of the previous (Offer -> Leads -> Money Models), written originally as one work and intentionally parsed into sequential volumes.

name: Logic-Evidence-Utility Problem Definition
definition: A three-question diagnostic sequence for validating any problem statement: What does it mean? How do you know? Why do I care? Forces specificity before prescribing solutions.

name: Good Friction vs Bad Friction
definition: A qualification framework distinguishing friction that removes bad-fit leads and improves sales efficiency (good friction) from friction that blocks all leads indiscriminately (bad friction). Adding good friction increases ROAS and sales efficiency simultaneously.

name: Checklist-Driven Sales Ops
definition: A complete list of sales operations requirements (scripting, daily training, cultural enforcement, manager accountability) where executing the full checklist produces consistent results and any gap identifies the leader as the constraint.

name: Group-Audition Hiring Funnel
definition: A four-stage sales-rep hiring process: send script before interview, request a 60-second video audition, invite finalists to a group drill call for coachability scoring, then make job offers only to those who pass all stages.

name: Distribution Node Acquisition Model
definition: Instead of tracking end-customer acquisition, count the number of distribution partners (agencies, producers) onboarded per month, treating each as a recurring referral node whose compounding output replaces the episodic churn of direct customer sales.

name: Two-Sided Education Monetization
definition: For certification test-prep businesses, monetize both sides of the supply-demand curve: charge students for test prep and charge universities/employers for career placement, so the same student cohort generates two revenue streams.

name: Brand Pairing Flywheel
definition: A four-step loop: start with a brand that means nothing, pair it deliberately with people or experiences ideal customers like, the brand inherits that meaning, customers buy to associate themselves with that meaning.

name: Advertising vs. Branding Distinction
definition: Advertising is letting people know about your product (reach/awareness); branding is the pairing and meaning that occurs as a result of that exposure. Good advertising can produce bad branding if the pairing is disliked by the target audience.

name: Consumable Input Framework
definition: A community offer has two separable components: (1) a one-time method/knowledge transfer and (2) recurring consumable inputs that the community must refresh each period. Retention and recurring revenue attach to the consumable layer, not to the knowledge layer.

name: Five Business Advantages Framework
definition: A five-factor scoring system (Sticky, Expensive, Expanding, Air/Low-OpEx, Unique) for evaluating and ranking the quality of a business opportunity vehicle; having even one advantage makes a business better than peers.

name: Roles-to-Workflows Shift
definition: Replace role-based hiring decisions (I need an editor) with workflow-based decomposition (I need an agent for task X, an agent for task Y) by mapping every business action at its most granular level until no further reduction is possible.

name: Linear Business Workflow Map
definition: A single continuous workflow diagram that traces every action in the business from first input (idea, inbound lead) to final output (posted content, closed deal); if you cannot draw it, the business lacks operational clarity.

name: Physics-as-Upper-Bound Standard
definition: Treat physical laws as the only legitimate ceiling on what a business can achieve; all other limitations are industry conventions that a sufficiently determined actor can override.

name: Attack-Vector Multiplicity Standard
definition: Measure the quality of someone's standards by how many distinct approaches they deploy toward a single goal before declaring it impossible; breadth of attack vectors proxies the height of standards.

name: Logic-Evidence-Utility Problem-Solving Framework
