definition: A three-question sequence for breaking down any problem: (1) What does this mean? (logic/definition), (2) How do we know? (evidence), (3) Why does it matter? (utility). Applied before any claim or diagnosis is accepted.

name: Any-Answer-Except-Call-Me Operator Test
definition: A role-play interview used to verify a GM candidate can handle operational crises (upset customer, facility emergency) without escalating to the owner; any response except calling the owner is the right answer.

name: Sell Warm Like Cold
definition: Apply the full cold-prospect qualification and credentialing process to every sale regardless of relationship warmth; warm prospects receive the same structured pitch as cold ones.
concept: Warm vs. Cold Sales Process Mismatch

name: Three-Option Objection Routing
definition: When a service objection blocks a client segment, route through three options in order: (1) sell through the objection, (2) redesign delivery to remove the objection, (3) restrict the target audience to those who lack the objection.

name: Fix-Then-Scale Sequencing
definition: Optimize unit economics and margins in existing locations before opening new ones; expansion funded by proven profitability, not ambition.

name: Sales Motion to Membership to Talent Ladder
definition: Three-stage repair sequence for stalled multi-location service businesses: (1) fix sales motion, (2) install membership model to maximize revenue per technician, (3) raise pay and build career paths to retain talent — in that order.

name: Affiliate Launch and Integration
definition: Two-stage affiliate activation: Launch (get 10 referrals fast to create an internal case study) followed by Integration (embed the provider into the affiliate's standard onboarding so 100% of new clients flow through, not just whoever the affiliate chooses to refer).

name: Behavioral Definition of Learning
definition: Learning is operationally defined as same condition producing new behavior; if condition is held constant and behavior does not change, no learning has occurred regardless of content consumed.

name: Deprivation-Reference-Point Model of Motivation
definition: Motivation equals the inverse of perceived deprivation; for intangible constructs like money, deprivation is measured relative to a reference group, not absolute scarcity.

name: Replicate Before Iterate
definition: A two-phase learning discipline: in phase one, follow instructions exactly and replicate the teacher's results without modification; in phase two, only after replication is confirmed, apply accumulated knowledge to diverge and derive personal improvements.

name: Pareto Wealth Distribution Model
definition: Alex Hormozi maps U.S. household net worth as $100 across 100 percentiles to show that 1% of the population holds $32, more than the bottom 90% combined, and uses this to guide customer targeting decisions.

name: Four-Tier Pricing Ladder
definition: A pricing architecture with four tiers separated by 5–10x price jumps, expecting approximately 20% of customers to ascend each tier; each tier is designed to roughly double total revenue.
concept: Pareto Pricing: Four-Tier Structure at 5–10x Gaps

name: Close Rate as Pricing Diagnostic
definition: Alex Hormozi's rubric for diagnosing pricing power from observed close rates: 60–80% indicates 3–4x underpricing, 50–60% indicates 1.5–2x, 40–50% indicates 1.25–1.5x, 30–40% is correctly priced, below 30% indicates a sales or qualification problem.
concept: Close Rate as Pricing Calibration Signal

name: Prospect-Driven Sales Process
definition: A scalable sales structure in which the salesperson asks questions in a prescribed order to lead the prospect to their own purchase decision, rather than demonstrating expertise or authority.

name: Top-Down Business Launch Sequence
definition: Start with a high-price, limited-volume premium offering; use early profits and brand equity to fund progressively higher-volume, lower-price tiers; never enter from the bottom and attempt to move up.

name: ROAS Doom Loop
definition: A self-reinforcing trap in which DTC brands keep increasing ad spend to reach less profitable audiences, compressing margins further until revenue equals spend with no net gain.

name: CRO as Steroids
definition: Conversion-rate optimization yields a one-time lift (sometimes 2-3x) but establishes a new baseline rather than resolving the structural front-end traffic and brand deficit.

name: SPCL Influence Framework
definition: A four-component model for building content-based influence: Status (controlling scarce reinforcers), Power (say-do correspondence that produces positive outcomes), Credibility (observable third-party evidence), and Likeness (shared values, aesthetics, or demographic similarity). Components stack multiplicatively — each one independently creates influence, all four together create maximal compliance likelihood.

name: Product-as-Marketing Framework
definition: In businesses where discovery is dominated by word-of-mouth and reviews (e.g., restaurants), product quality and experience ARE the marketing spend; premium investment in ingredients, service, and environment replaces advertising as the primary acquisition lever.

name: Grand Opening Playbook
definition: A staged launch protocol for new physical locations: overstaff, run multi-channel ads, and deploy promotional giveaways (or exclusive invite-only events for premium concepts) to generate sufficient initial word-of-mouth so the location reaches full capacity within six months organically.

name: Sales Ops Checklist
definition: A complete list of sales operations requirements — daily training, script memorization, script enforcement, and manager accountability — that together produce consistent close rates; missing any items and you do not get the outcome.

name: Group Screening Funnel for Sales Reps
definition: A three-stage hiring filter for sales reps: (1) send script before interview and request 60-second video recitation; (2) invite passing candidates to a group coaching call and evaluate coachability under live feedback; (3) extend job offers only to those who demonstrated preparation and coachability.

name: Three Traits of Hyper-Successful People
definition: Hyper-successful people share three co-occurring traits: (1) grandiose vision with an inflated superiority complex, (2) crippling insecurity where they feel they will never be good enough, and (3) strong impulse control enabling sustained focus over extended periods.

name: Input-Output Equation
definition: A two-step framework: (1) define the specific input action that produces your desired output, then (2) multiply that input by 10x or 100x to reach the corresponding outcome at scale.

name: Three Levels of Leverage Stack
definition: A three-tier model illustrating increasing leverage on a single input action: Level 1 = do the action yourself, Level 2 = hire someone to do the action for you (same output, no founder effort), Level 3 = hire a recruiter who brings a new doer each week so the input compounds indefinitely without additional founder effort.

name: Sequential Control Relinquishment
definition: A staged model in which founders must surrender control of business functions in a fixed sequence to unlock leverage at each stage: delivery/production, admin, sales, marketing, management, leadership, financing, and finally the need to be needed by the business.

name: Paid Ads More Sequence
definition: Three-step risk-ordered sequence for scaling paid advertising: (1) make more creative, (2) spend more money, (3) expand to more platforms — each step unlocks the next.

name: Count in Hundreds Framework
definition: Measure depth of mastery by counting effort in units of hundreds of hours rather than hours; the shift in unit of measurement forces a qualitatively different level of engagement with the subject.

name: Done When Nothing More Can Be Improved
definition: Work is declared complete not when time runs out but when the producer cannot identify any further change that would make the output better; this redefines done from time-based to quality-based.

name: Work Works on You More Than You Work on It
definition: The output of sustained effort is not only the external result but the transformation of the person doing the work; the worker becomes the primary asset produced.

name: Mountain Climbing Capacity Metaphor
definition: Smaller mountains must be climbed before larger ones; each climb builds the capacity and belief needed to attempt the next, higher mountain. Maps to: small wins -> compounding capability -> readiness for bigger challenges.

name: Inverse PTSD Framework
definition: Each survived hardship serves as exposure therapy, raising the experiential floor of what you know you can handle; subsequent challenges feel survivable because prior evidence confirms you did not die.

name: Two-Vector Learning Framework
definition: Intelligence can be improved on two independent axes: reducing the number of iterations required to change behavior, OR compressing the time between iterations so the total elapsed time to behavior change is lower.

name: Skill as Chained Behaviors
definition: A skill is a sequence of individually learned same-condition/new-behavior pairs chained together; each link in the chain is a discrete learning event.

name: Consumable Media Framework
definition: A three-part model for membership businesses: sell the system (education) once up front, then supply the monthly consumable inputs that make the system work, reducing churn by ensuring members need the product every single month.

name: Three Levels of Accountability
definition: Peer-to-peer (shoulder-to-shoulder with someone at the same stage), alumni (one step ahead, cheerleading), and expert (has solved the problem hundreds of times) accountability layers sold as a retention and value-delivery system inside membership offers.

name: Ink-Printer Revenue Model
definition: Sell the machine (system, education) once; sell the ink (consumable inputs) repeatedly every month. The initial one-time sale enables a recurring revenue stream from the supplies the machine requires.
